articles.headline: Antoine Semenyo says Ghana players want to 'prove a point' against England at FIFA World Cup
articles.description: After their dramatic 1-0 victory over Panama on Wednesday night, Antoine Semenyo says Ghana will head into their FIFA World Cup showdown with England looking to 'prove a point'.

images.name: Antoine Semenyo [1296x729]
images.caption: Antoine Semenyo was the picture of joy when Ghana scored during injury time to beat Panama at the FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Semenyo: Ghana want to 'prove a point' vs. England
images.caption: Semenyo: Ghana want to 'prove a point' vs. England

articles.headline: England vs. Ghana: Kick-off time, team news, how to watch FIFA World Cup clash
articles.description: Ghana under Carlos Queiroz have many players who will be a known quantity for England, but the Black Stars pose a threat at the World Cup.

images.name: Semenyo: Ghana want to 'prove a point' vs. England
images.caption: Semenyo: Ghana want to 'prove a point' vs. England

articles.headline: Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi credits 'intensity' in training for Black Stars success
articles.description: Ghana's teenage midfielder Caleb Yirenkyi says his stoppage time goal, which helped Ghana secure a valuable FIFA World Cup win over Panama, was the product of weeks of intense work on the training ground.

images.name: Caleb Yirenkyi [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Yirenkyi attends Ghana training at the FIFA World Cup.
images.credit: Ezra Shaw - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

articles.headline: Re-ranking Africa's World Cup teams after their opening matches
articles.description: With everyone having played once, how have their performances stacked up against our original list, and what does a revised ranking look like now?

images.caption: Cape Verde dented Spain's FIFA World Cup title ambition.
images.alt: Vozinha #1 of Cabo Verde applaud fans after the 0-0 draw during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match between Spain and Cabo Verde at Atlanta Stadium on June 15, 2026 in Atlanta, Georgia.

images.alt: Cedric Bakambu #17 of DR Congo leaps on top of teammates to celebrate a goal by Yoane Wissa #20 of DR Congo during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Portugal and Congo DR on June 17, 2026, at Houston Stadium in Houston, Texas.

articles.headline: Tunisia must be 'collectively perfect' against Japan says emergency head coach Herve Renard
articles.description: Tunisia will take inspiration from Cape Verde's draw with Spain as they look to launch their FIFA World Cup salvage act against Japan in Monterrey on Saturday, according to newly appointed head coach Herve Renard.

images.name: Herve Renard [1296x729]
images.caption: Herve Renard speaks at the press conference ahead of Tunisia vs Japan.
images.credit: Julio Cesar AGUILAR / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Why did Tunisia hire Herve Renard to replace Sabri Lamouchi?
images.caption: Why did Tunisia hire Herve Renard to replace Sabri Lamouchi?

articles.headline: From Ayyoub Bouaddi to Yan Diomande - Which players are raising their transfer profiles at the FIFA World Cup?
articles.description: Nothing can change the trajectory of a player's career quite like a strong showing at the FIFA World Cup, and a number of players have already raised their profiles in the opening salvos.

images.name: Yan Diomande, Ivory Coast vs Ecuador [1296x729]
images.caption: Ivory Coast youngster Yan Diomande is rumoured to be on the move from RB Leipzig, to either PSG or Liverpool.
images.alt: Yan Diomande #11 of Cote d'Ivoire celebrates as Amad Diallo #15 (not pictured) scores the team's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E match between Cote D'Ivoire and Ecuador at Philadelphia Stadium on June 14, 2026 in Philadelphia.
images.credit: Ezra Shaw - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Brahim Diaz [1296x729]
images.caption: Brahim Diaz of Real Madrid and Morocco could be on the move to Juventus, according to reports.

images.name: Onuoha praises Diomande's impact in Ivory Coast's win over Ecuador
images.caption: Onuoha praises Diomande's impact in Ivory Coast's win over Ecuador

articles.headline: World Cup 2026: Why do Japan fans clean up the stadium?
articles.description: Japan supporters have built a respected reputation for tidying up football stadiums before they leave. But what is the reason for it?

images.name: japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan fans have tidied stadiums since their first World Cup in 1998.

articles.headline: William Troost-Ekong backs Ghana for surprise World Cup draw with England
articles.description: William Troost-Ekong wants Ghana to pull off a FIFA World Cup shock against England despite Nigeria's rivalry with the Black Stars.

images.name: William Troost-Ekong [1296x729]
images.caption: William Troost-Ekong admitted that it is easier to wish Ghana success now at the FIFA World Cup than it would have been when he was still Nigeria captain.
images.credit: Ulrik Pedersen/DeFodi Images via Getty Images
